title: Easy Auto Restart
description: Adds auto restarting to Minecraft servers.
body:
# Easy Auto Restart

Easy Auto Restart is a lightweight Fabric mod designed to add simple automatic restarting capabilities to your Minecraft server. It focuses on doing one thing well: managing restarts and executing your start script when the server stops.

## Commands

All commands require Operator (OP) permissions.

*   `/restart`
    *   Stops the server immediately and triggers the restart script.
*   `/cancelrestart`
    *   Cancels any currently running restart countdown.
*   `/easyautorestart restart`
    *   Initiates a restart sequence with a 1-minute countdown.
*   `/easyautorestart add <HH:mm>`
    *   Adds a new daily auto-restart time (e.g., `/easyautorestart add 04:00`).
*   `/easyautorestart remove <HH:mm>`
    *   Removes an existing daily restart time.
*   `/easyautorestart list`
    *   Displays all currently scheduled daily restart times.

## Configuration

 The configuration file is generated at `config/easy-auto-restart.json`. You can edit this file directly or use the in-game commands to manage schedules.

```json
{
  "dailyRestartTimes": [
    "04:00",
    "16:00"
  ],
  "scriptPath": "./start.sh",
  "executeScript": true
}
```

*   **dailyRestartTimes**: A list of times (24-hour format) when the server should automatically restart.
*   **scriptPath**: The path to the script that launches your server.
    *   *Windows users should point this to their `.bat` file (e.g., `./start.bat`).*
    *   *Linux users should point this to their `.sh` file (e.g., `./start.sh`).*
*   **executeScript**: If set to `true`, the mod attempts to run the file at `scriptPath` when the server stops. Set to `false` if you only want the mod to stop the server (e.g., if you use an external wrapper like Pterodactyl or Docker).

## Installation & Setup

1.  Move the `.jar` file into your server's `mods` folder.
2.  Start the server once to generate the config.
3.  **Important**: Ensure you have a valid start script (`start.bat` or `start.sh`) in your server's root directory. The mod relies on this script to bring the server back up after it stops.
